title: Intro to economics: Scarcity
description: This exercise identifies Adam Smith, defines scarcity, effects of economics on personal planning

assessment_items.question_content:
Among historical figures who have made significant contributions to economics, Adam Smith holds a notable place. 

**Which of the following best describes who Adam Smith is?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a renowned physicist from Scotland
a popular actor from the 18th century
a Scottish philosopher known as the first economist
the first president of an economic association

assessment_items.question_content:
Among historical figures who have made significant contributions to economics, Adam Smith is particularly famous.

**What is Adam Smith best known for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: developing the theory of classical economics
creating the first modern bank
founding the first stock exchange
the first president of an economic association

assessment_items.question_content:
In the history of economic thought, Adam Smith is a standout figure.

**Which of the following best describes one of Adam Smith’s contributions to economic theory?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pioneering the idea that self-interest can lead to positive societal outcomes
creating the first stock market
founding the concept of microeconomics
developing the principles of socialism

assessment_items.question_content:
Scarcity is a fundamental concept in economics that influences decision-making and resource allocation.

**What does it mean to say that a resource is scarce?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A society does not have enough money to purchase this resource.
Nobody wants to buy this resource.
There is not enough of this resource to satisfy all the wants and needs of a society.
There is an unlimited amount of the resource available.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scarcity plays a crucial role in shaping economic policies and priorities.

**Which of the following describes a scarce resource?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a demand for a resource with limited supply.
The resource has unlimited supply and limited demand.
There is a fixed quantity of the resource available but no demand for it.
The supply of the resource exceeds the demand of the resource.

assessment_items.question_content:
Understanding scarcity is essential for analyzing how markets operate and resources are distributed.

**How does a resource become scarce?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when there is less of it
when more people want it
when governments ban it
when producers limit the supply of it

assessment_items.question_content:
Economic principles such as scarcity and microeconomics shape our financial decisions. 

**How do these concepts directly affect personal financial planning?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It lets us understand trends in world stock markets to make investment decisions.
It helps us understand why governments make rules about trading with other countries.
It guides us to make smart decisions about spending and saving, keeping in mind things are not always available.
It encourages us to seek high-level political roles to influence economic policies.

assessment_items.question_content:
A fundamental aspect of personal financial planning is understanding that we must manage our spending wisely, given money is not an unlimited resource.

**What economic concept is this scenario best explained by?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Invisible Hand
scarcity
resources
self-interest

assessment_items.question_content:
In our personal financial planning, we need to budget carefully to avoid spending money that we don't have.

**Which economic principle does the statement above reflect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: capitalism
self-interest
scarcity
the Invisible Hand

assessment_items.question_content:
Consumers, businesses, and government decision-makers all face scarcity of resources. 

**When managing scarcity, which resource is the government as a decision-maker primarily concerned with?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the national budget
policy on individual income tax
regulation of corporations
the implementation of a minimum wage

assessment_items.question_content:
Consumers, businesses, and government decision-makers all face scarcity of resources. 

**Which aspect of resource scarcity is an individual most likely to encounter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: limitations on personal income
the decision to vote in national or local elections
choice of employment or profession
pursuit of higher education or vocational training

assessment_items.question_content:
Consumers, businesses, and government decision-makers all face scarcity of resources. 

**Within the business environment, which resource most commonly represents a challenge due to scarcity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: available capital or raw materials
decisions relating to branding and marketing
choices around business location or expansion
strategies for customer engagement and retention
